Empires & Puzzles Ommodus Review: The Lawbringer of Owl Tower

Ommodus brings the law with a massive axe and a severe hatred for Holy heroes. But in a world of supersonic speeds, does this slow Owl still have enough authority to enforce the rules? We break down the math behind his Moonrise Strike.
Ommodus: The Judge, Jury, and Executioner (Who Arrives Late)

Ommodus is a 5-star Dark (Purple) hero from the Owl Tower event, originally released in late 2023. As a member of the Owl family, he brings the unique "Moonrise Strike" mechanic, which promises massive damage potential based on stat differences. He is a Slow mana Barbarian, casting a wide net of destruction—or at least, that was the plan. In the current fast-paced meta, he often feels like a judge who shows up to the courtroom after the verdict has already been read.

Ommodus features a striking design—a humanoid owl clad in Roman-esque armor, wielding a massive poleaxe. The purple feathers and stern expression fit his "Lawbringer" persona perfectly. It's a majestic design that screams authority, even if his in-game speed suggests he's taking a leisurely flight to the battlefield.
Let's be blunt: Ommodus is showing his age. With a base Attack of
, he is significantly behind modern 5-star heroes who now regularly push

heroes (like the costumed Christmas elves) now rival his durability. This stat deficit is critical because his special skill relies entirely on the difference between his Attack and the enemy's Defense. Against modern tanks with 1300+ Defense, Ommodus struggles to deal his maximum potential damage without significant help.

He destroys ALL Fiends from ALL allies. This is an excellent utility, especially against heavy fiend summoners.

Upon release, Ommodus was seen as a "whale's toy" for Rush Wars. He was powerful but overshadowed by faster heroes. The "Moonrise" mechanic was new and confusing, and many players underestimated the damage potential against weaker teams.
The game has moved on. The stat gap is undeniable. His mechanics (Fiend destroy, mana reduction) are timeless, but his delivery system (stats + speed) is outdated.

Ommodus needs to survive to fire, but his damage scales off Attack. This creates a dilemma.

If you are using Ommodus, you are using him to wipe the board in Rush or counter specific setups. If he survives but hits for 300 damage, he's useless. You have to go all-in on Attack and rely on other heroes (taunt, revival) to keep him alive.

To make Ommodus work in 2026, you need to manipulate the stat differential.

Any hero that lowers Ommodus's attack (e.g., Scarlett, various Barbarians) effectively neuters his damage formula.
While he resists mana ailments, direct mana cuts or silence can still stop him.

If you pulled Ommodus early, he is a great "nuke" for map stages and lower-level raids. Don't worry about the complex math; just know that he hits everyone hard, especially yellow monsters. Level him, but prioritize faster heroes for your main team later.

He's a luxury tool for your 4th or 5th War team, specifically for Rush or anti-Fiend duties. Do not limit break him (LB2) unless you are drowning in alphas and love Rush wars. There are far better Dark heroes for your primary resources.
The community has always had a love-hate relationship with the Owls.
“All slow fiend removers are complete garbage,”
one player lamented upon his release, comparing him to the infamous "Kettle."
Another user noted the "poisoned luck" of pulling him multiple times while chasing featured heroes.
“In Rush, he wipes out entire teams single-handedly.”

Ommodus is a hero out of time. He has the kit of a god-slayer but the stats of a mortal. In the niche world of Rush Attack or against Fiend-heavy teams, he can still bring down the gavel with authority. Everywhere else, he’s mostly just a very angry, very slow bird.
Keep for Rush, bench for everything else.
A deep dive into Ommodus, the 5-star Dark Barbarian from the Owl family. Is his slow Moonrise Strike still worth it in the current meta? We analyze his stats, skills, and viability.
